Localization.Description: Windows Registry abuse. This is often used by attackers for persistence, privilege escalation, lateral movement, defense evasion, data collection, and other malicious activity.

EventDescriptions.EventDescription: Process {subject.process.name} added Base64-encoded registry key value "{object.name}" on host {event_src.host}. This indicates bypassing protection with intent to execute the encoded file.

Process {subject.process.name} added a registry key value "{object.name}" that has the structure of a Base64 encoded message, but with an invalid decoding result on host {event_src.host}. This indicates bypassing protection with intent to execute the encoded file.
Process {subject.process.name} added a very long registry key value "{object.name}" on host {event_src.host}. This indicates bypassing protection with intent to execute the encoded file.
Artifacts in registry key "{object.name}" injected by process {subject.process.name} indicate possible {datafield1} attack on host {event_src.host}
